label: Cardiomegaly
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT (Updated)
Findings:
There is mild rotation to the left.
The heart is enlarged (CTR - 0.55) with a left ventricular configuration.
Increased pulmonary vasculature with fluffy opacities and air bronchogram sign, in both lung fields predominantly in the perihilar regions, middle and lower lung zones
There is blunting of the costophrenic sulci and hemidiaphragms by a homogenous opacity (left >> right).
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
Impression:
Pneumonic consolidation and pulmonary oedema with bilateral pleural effusion 2o cardiac decompensation
ECG / Echo advised for further evaluation.

report_text:

The right lung field is clear.
The mediastinum, both hila and pulmonary vasculature are also normal.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
CONCLUSION
Left moderate pleural effusion - on chest tube.
In comparison with the previous image, no significant improvement.

label: Device_Present
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Clinical Information: Trauma
Findings:
There is hyper-transradiancy with loss of lung markings at the apical region of the right hemithorax,
Non-segmental patchy opacities seen in both lung fields, suggestive of pulmonary contusions.
A homogenous opacity with meniscus sign noted in the left cardiophrenic angle. There is associated blunting of the right costophrenic sulcus.
Cardiac and mediastinal shadows are within normal limits.
Endotracheal tube noted with tip in the right main bronchus.
Chest tube noted in-situ with its tip at the right 4th intercostal space.
Soft tissue irregularity and emphysema noted at the right lateral chest wall, ? iatrogenic
A cortical defect noted at the inferior margin of the left posterior 8th rib. The superior margin is intact, raising the suspicion of chip fracture.
Lateral margins of the left clavicle and inferior portions of the 12th rib are not visualized.
Impression
Polytraumatized patient with:

Right pneumothorax
Bilateral hemothorax
Wrongly placed endotracheal tube
Pulmonary contusions
Left 8th rib chip fracture

label: Cardiomegaly
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
There is cardiomegaly of biventricular configuration (CTR 0.57)
There are fractures involving the lateral wing of the left scapular, posterior 6th rib, and anterior 3rd rib. With adjacent soft tissue swelling and emphysema.  There is opacity of metallic density noted in the surrounding soft tissue ? iodinated antiseptic. There is fairly rounded ill-defined  inhomogeneous opacity of soft tissue density noted in the left middle and part of the upper lung zones and shows central lytic area suggestive of pulmonary contusion and loculated pulmonary hematoma. There is associated subtle compression of left cardiac margin, mediastinal shift and tracheal shift to contralateral side.
The right lung field appears preserved.
Both costophrenic recesses are free.
The remaining ribs and clavicles appear preserved.
FINDINGS:
Pulmonary contusion with loculated hematoma with possible cardiac tamponade
Left rib fractures
Left scapular fracture
Cardiomegaly
Kindly correlate with other clinical parameters.

report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH  REPORT

The bones of the thorax appear normal.
There is increased density of the soft tissue over the right lateral chest wall inkeeping with edema.
Conclusion: 
Right Pleural effusion with underlying atelectasis

label: Atelectasis
report_text:
LUMBOSACRAL SPINE RADIOGRAPHY REPORT
There is straightening of the lumbar spine, with lumbarization of S1 vertebral body
There is posterior listhesis of 25% of L1 on L2.
Osteophytes are seen marginally on L3 to L5 and  anteriorly on L2 to L5 vertebral bodies.
There is narrowing of L2/L3, L3/L4, L4/L5 disc spaces with adjacent end plate sclerosis
75% collapse of L4 vertebral body noted.
Posterior elements are within normal limits.
The sacrum and sacroiliac joint spaces are within normal limits
The pre and paravertebral soft tissue is normal
Finding:  Lumbar Spondylosis with L4 vertebral planar
Grade 1 posterolisthesis of L1 on L2 vertebral bodies.

label: Atelectasis
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Comments
Tubal structure with tip is noted in the region of the left 6th posterior intercostal space.
There is crescentic lucency devoid of bronchovascular markings noted in the peripheral aspect of the left lung field with associated reduction in the volume of the ipsilateral lung tissue, suggestive of atelectasis. There is associated ill-defined opacities of soft tissue density in the left lower lung zone.
The right lung field is preserved.
Thoracic cage is within normal limits.
There are molted opacities noted in the adjacent ipsilateral soft tissue, suggestive of surgical emphysema.
Multiple fairly rounded opacity of calcific density noted, suggestive of bullet pallet fragment.
Impression
Left pneumothorax atelectasis.

label: Atelectasis
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Tubal artifact with the tip noted in the region of the second intercostal space of the left hemithorax
There is inhomogenous opacity noted in the left lower zone which is seen silhouetting the left hemidiaphragm and part of the left cardiac border. There is associated crowding of the left pulmonary vessels.
The left upper lung zone shows compensatory hypertransradiance and the right lung field appears preserved.
Opacities of metallic densities are noted in the left hemithorax and ipsilateral soft tissue shadow suggestive of bullet pellets.
The bony thorax is within normal limits.
Impression:
Features are suggestive of segmental atelectasis secondary to gunshot injury

Features in keeping with pulmonary kochs

Impression:
Features in keeping with pulmonary kochs

label: Atelectasis
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
There is homogenous opacity noted in the right lower lung zone with irregular outline silhouetting the right hemidiaphragm and right cardiac border.
Inhomogenous opacity of soft tissue density with air-bronchogram is noted above the homogenous opacity with associated fissural thickening, slight caudal displacement of the transverse fissure and tracheal deviation to the ipslateral side, suggestive of possible pulmonary fibrosis and collapse.
Streaky opacities are noted in the lower left lung zone.
The heart size is indeterminate, however the aorta appears unfolded.
The left costophrenic sulci and hemidiaphragm are preserved.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
CONCLUSION
Features are suggestive of right pleural effusion with pneumonic consolidation and possible pulmonary fibrosis and collapse, rule out pulmonary TB

Post chest tube insertion
Comparison
No previous exams available.

Right-sided pleural effusion with left lung atelectasis

label: Cardiomegaly
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
The heart is enlarged (CTR=0.60), with left ventricular configuration. The outline is however smooth.
There is some unfolding of the aorta, with prominence of its knuckle. The hila and pulmonary vasculature are normal.
There are fibrotic streaks in both upper and lower lung fields tenting the hemidiaphragms.
Both costophrenic sulcus and hemidiaphragms are preserved.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
CONCLUSION
Cardiomegaly.
Suggest ECG/ Echocardiography for further evaluation.
Possibility of Pulmonary Tuberculosis with fibrotic streaks.
GeneXpert advised for confirmation

label: Device_Present
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Inhomogenous opacity noted in both lower and middle lung zones silhouetting the left cardiac border and the left hemidiaphragm. Pulmonary effusion with meniscus sign and lamellar tracking is noted up to the 6th right intercostal space.There is generalised reticular shadowing with some noularities noted involving the remaining lung zones bilaterally.
Tubal artifact noted in the left lung field with the tip at the 4th posterior intercostal space.
The heart is normal in size and contour.
The mediastinum, hila and pulmonary vasculature are also normal.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
Impression
Lymphangitis carcinomatosis with bilateral pleural effusion in keeping with pulmonary metastasis from the clinically diagnosed breast cancer.

Hypertransradiance of the lower left lung zone is seen, devoid of lung markings, with consequent passive collapse of the lower left lung lobe.
There is associated deviation of the heart and mediastinum to the right, and enlargement of the left hemithorax and flattening of the left hemidiaphragm.
There is haziness of the right lung field, likely due to rotation. There is also crowding of both lung markings.
The heart is normal in size and contour.
The right costophrenic sulcus and hemidiaphragm are preserved.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
CONCLUSION
Left Tension Pneumothorax as evidenced by flattening of the left hemidiaphragm and displaced mediastinum, in background pulmlonary TB
Correlate with chest CT if clinically indicated.

label: Atelectasis
report_text:
AP CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Clinical information: Disseminated TB
Comparison: Chest Xray 04/06/23
Findings:
Previously identified left-sided pneumothorax has resolved with subsequent re-expansion of the ipsilateral lung tissue.
There is however areas of hypertransradiancy devioid of lung markings noted at the periphery of the right hemithorax, indicative of pneumothorax.
Widespread recticular opacities noted in both lung fields
The cardiac and mediastinal shadows are within normal limits.
Chest tube is seen projected over the left hemithorax with its tip at the level of T11 vertebra.
Soft tissue irregularity and dressing artifact noted at the lateral chest walls bilaterally.
The costophrenic sulci and hemidiaphragms are preserved.
The bony rib cage is intact.
Impression:
Resolved left tension pneumothorax with re-expansion of left lung parenchyma, in a patient with background pulmonary TB
Mild right pneumothorax

Unfolded aorta, ? age-related
Elevated right hemidiaphragm, ? 2o right middle lobe collapse
Ultrasound advised

label: Cardiomegaly
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Left rotation of the radiograph noted.
Air space homogeneous opacity is noted in the right perihilar and right pericardiac region with air bronchogram in keeping with consolidations.  
Patchy air space opacities are also seen in the left perihilar region.
The heart is enlarged (CTR=0.57), with left ventricular multichamber involvement. The outline is however smooth.
There is some unfolding of the aorta, with prominence of its knuckle.
The pulmonary vessels are obscured by the consolidation.
There is blunting of both costophrenic sulci.
Both hemidiaphragms are preserved.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
Central venous line is noted with the tip in the region of the superior vena cava.
CONCLUSION
Features are suggestive of Cardiomegaly with pulmonary edema with underlying pneumonic changes.
ECHO is advised.

label: Cardiomegaly
evidence_phrases: cardiomegaly; cardiac silhouette is enlarged; ctr=60%
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Findings:
There is a homogenous opacity noted at the right lower lung zone with air-fluid level silhouetting the right hemidiaphragm suggestive of a right residual  pleural effusion.
There is an area of lucency noted in the right lower lung zone devoid of lung markings suggestive of  a pneumothorax likely iatrogenic
Tubal artifact (thoracotomy tube) is noted with the tip in the left 8th intercostal space and above the left lung field (likely oxygen delivery tubing)
There are reticulonodular opacities noted  diffusely in both lung fields
There are also  areas of calcifc foci noted in the lower lung zones bilaterallylikely old granuloma .
The cardiac silhouette is enlarged(CTR=60%) with left ventricular configuration
Aortic knuckle unfolding  calcification is noted.
The bones of the thorax appear normal.
Impression
Resolving right pleural effusion with iatrogenic right hydropneumothorax
Cardiomegaly
Reticulononudar density in both lung field-Possibility of lymphagitis carcinomatosis 

Conclusion: Known case of carcinoma of the cervix with possibility of chest metastasis.
CT chest is advised if clinically indicated.

label: Cardiomegaly
evidence_phrases: cardiac shadow appears enlarged; cardiac failure
report_text:
AP CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Comparison: Chest Xray 20/09/23
Findings:
Rotated radiograph.
There is interval improvement relative to the volume of the previously identified effusion.
The cardiac shadow appears enlarged with aortic unfolding.
There is upper lobe diversion.
Fibrotic streaks noted at right lower lung zone.
Curvilinear calcification of the aortic knob is noted, consistent with atherosclerosis.
Central venous line is seen projected over the right hemithorax with tip in the SVC.
Chest tube is also seen on the right hemithorax with tip at the level of right posterior 5th rib, proximal to the level of the effusion
Soft tissue irregularity and dressing artifact seen on the right lateral chest wall.
The extrathoracic bony structures show evidence of degenerative changes.
Impression:
Features suggestive of cardiac failure with residual right pleural effusion
CT advised

There is upper lobe diversion.
Fibrotic streaks noted at right lower lung zone.
Curvilinear calcification of the aortic knob is noted, consistent with atherosclerosis.
Central venous line is seen projected over the right hemithorax with tip in the SVC.
Chest tube is also seen on the right hemithorax with tip at the level of right posterior 5th rib, proximal to the level of the effusion
Soft tissue irregularity and dressing artifact seen on the right lateral chest wall.
The extrathoracic bony structures show evidence of degenerative changes.
Impression:
Features suggestive of cardiac failure with residual right pleural effusion
CT advised

label: Cardiomegaly
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
There are fairly rounded soft tissue density of varying sizes noted in both lung fields, but predominantly in the left; in keeping with cannon ball. 
There is diffuse reticular opacity with some nodular infiltration noted in both lung fields. 
Transverse fissural thickening is noted.
There is  veil like homogeneous opacity with laminar tracking noted in both lower lung zones with silhouette of the cardiophrenic sulci (Improved compared to previous radiograph on 23/09/2023)
The heart is enlarged, CTR 0.54.
The costophrenic sulci and hemidiaphragms are obscured.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
Chest tube is noted with the tip projecting over the right cardiac border.
Findings
Cannon ball metastasis
Cardiomegaly
Transverse fissural thickening
Bilateral pleural effusion
CONCLUSION
Overall features are those of metastatic lung disease secondary to clinically diagnosed breast cancer
Cardiomegaly
ECG/ECHO is advised
Kindly correlate with other clinical parameters.

report_text:

Hypertransradiant area devoid of lung markings noted at the upper region of the right hemithorax, indicative of pneumothorax.
There is reduction in the right lung volume with associated ipsilateral mediastinal shift and compensatory hyperinflation of the left lung.
Cardiac size is within normal limits.
The left costophrenic sulcus and hemidiaphragm is preserved.
The bony rib cage is normal.
Impression:
Moderate right hydropneumothorax with underlying lung collapse

label: Atelectasis
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Widespread inhomogenous opacities over both lung fields (left more than right )
A  Large air fluid level over the right lower lung zone   With a pencil thin line opacity over the lateral/peripheral region noted 
The cardiac shadow is normal in size 
The cardiothoracic ratio  is 49.5%
The diaphragm, left costophrenic angle and bony thorax are within normal 
Soft tissue emphysema noted  in the axillae and supraclavicular regions
Dressing artifact is noted over the left lateral chest wall.
Tubal artifact is noted in the right lung field, the tip is noted at the 2nd right intercostal space. Another tubal artifact is noted across the right lung field suggestive of oxygen delivery tubing.
Conclusion:
Features suggest Acute respiratory distress syndrome with right sided hydro pneumothorax and subcutaneous emphysema

Normal pulmonary vasculature is seen.
Both hilar angles are preserved.
The visualized bones of the thorax are normal.
Impression
Hypertensive heart disease with right lower lobe pneumonic consolidation.

report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Haziness of the right lung field is noted.
Homogenous opacity is noted in the right lower lung zone silhouetting the right hemidiaphragm and right cardiac border, suggestive of a pleural effusion. Mild interval improvement when compared with the previous radiograph (29/10/2023).
Chest tube artefact is noted with its tip abutting the mediastinum at the 4th right intercostal space.
The heart size is indeterminate.
The mediastinum is normal.
The left costophrenic sulcus and hemidiaphragm are preserved.
Surgical staple artefacts are noted overlying the right side of the chest wall. Air-lucencies are also noted in the right chest wall in keeping with subcutaneous emphysema, likely iatrogenic.
The bony rib cage appears normal.
CONCLUSION
Right moderate pleural effusion

There is homogeneous opacity noted in the medial aspect of the left upper lung zone with peripheral zone devoid of lung marking and associated subtle tracheal deviation to the ipsilateral side suggestive of effusion with concomitant segmental lung collapse. Right lung field appears preserved.
The bony rib cage and perpherial soft tissues are normal.
Chest tube noted with tip projected over the left clavicular medial head. 
Surgical clips projected over the left thoracic soft tissue

Left hydropneumothorax with pneumonic consolidation and atelectasis

Surgical clips artefacts are noted overlying the lower left chest wall.
The bony rib cage is normal.

Resolving post-pneumonectomy effusion

label: Atelectasis
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Inadequate radiograph- cutoff of the costophrenic angles.
There is a large area of hypertransradiance and absent lung markings in the lateral left lung field, suggestive of a pneumothorax. There is no associated tracheal deviation or flattening of the left hemidiaphragm.
There is a medial oblique linear opacity of soft tissue density with wide base at the lower left lung zone silhouetting the ipsilateral hemidiaphragm and cardiac margin suggestive of collapsed left lung segments.
There is an oval opacity with prominent periphery measuring 95 x 65mm noted in the left hilar region suggestive of eggshell calcified hilar lymphadenopathy ?cavitary lesion.
The right lung appears normal.
The heart appears normal in size and contour.
The mediastinum, hila and pulmonary vasculature are also normal.
The hemidiaphragms are preserved.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
CONCLUSION
Left lung collapse with pneumothorax rule out TB
Recommendations: lateral radiograph would be needed to locate apparent medisatinum lesion.

Recommend CT scan for further evaluation

There are multiple  fairly rounded opacities  of varying sizes noted in both lung fields, worse on the right. A patchy area of consolidation is noted it the upper right lung zone
The left costophrenic recess and hemidiaphragm are normal.
The visualized bones of the thorax are normal.
Extra pulmonary soft tissues are normal.
Findings :  
Bilateral intrapulmonary multi nodular opacities,  right sided areas of consolidation   and pleural effusion 
Differentials:
Atypical pneumonia with ?Respiratory bronchiolitis 
Pulmonary Kochs
Metastatic lesion cannot be ruled out .
Recommend CT scan for further evaluation

report_text:

Massive cardiomegaly
Prominence of the pulmonary bay
Cephalization
= Stage 2 Heart Failure
Aortic Unfolding
ECG, Echo advised

CT scan advised.

label: Atelectasis
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Comparison: 03/02/2024
There is near- homogenous opacification of the left hemithorax, with air-fluid level suggestive of hydropneumothorax, silhouetting the left cardiac border and hemidiaphragm. No interval improvement when compared to the last radiograph (03/02/24)
The trachea and mediastinum are mildly deviated to the left, suggestive of a left upper lobe atelectasis.
The heart size is indeterminate.
The right lung field is clear. No right pleural effusion.
Chest tube artefact is seen in the left hemithorax with its tip underlying the posterior part of the left 8th rib.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
CONCLUSION
Post airway toileting in a known left lung cancer patient with residual left pleural effusion (hydropneumothorax) and possible left lung collapse, with no interval improvement

Impression:
Right lobar pneumonia with complicated moderate right pleural effusion

label: Atelectasis
report_text:
Chest radiograph
Patient rotated to the right.
There is expansion of the right lung parenchyma with increased lucency when compared to the left and associated flattenening of the right hemidiaphragm. This suggests emphysematous changes
Right Upper zone fibrotic   Streaks noted with thickening and elevation of the minor fissure
There is streak opacity in the left perihilar  region. Remaining lung zones are normal.
Blunting of right costophrenic angle. The left costophrenic sulcus and hemidiaphragm are preserved.
The heart is normal in size and contour.
Mediastinum and hila are within normal limits.
Bony thorax is preserved.
Tubal artefact projected over right hemithorax with tip at the level of T12. Lateral chest wall soft tissue swelling containing air density subjacent to the region of tube insertion suggestive of soft tissue emphysema
Findings
No features of pulmonary metastasis.
Right lung emphysema
Right mild pleural effusion with thickened oblique fissure
Bilateral upper zone fibrotic streaks
Impression
Features are suggestive of Pulmonary TB
Clinical and laboratory correlation advised

label: Atelectasis
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
There is inhomogeneous opacification of the right hemithorax with multiple well-defined soft tissue density masses of varying sizes and shapes predominantly in the middle and lower lung zones.
Also, silhouette of ipsilateral hilar region and cardiac border noted. There is associated crowding of 2nd-7th right posterior ribs suggesting right lung volume reduction, however trachea is central.
Similar but smaller masses are randomly distributed in the left lung, and the ipsilateral hilar region is obscured.
Background veiled homogeneous opacity seen in the left hemithorax.
The heart size is indeterminate.
The left hila and pulmonary vasculature are normal.
The diaphragm and both cardiophrenic sulci are obscured.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
Findings
Multiple pulmonary masses bilaterally
Suggestion of right lung volume reduction
Bilateral pleural effusion (LT > RT)
CONCLUSION
Bilateral pulmonary metastasis with suggestion of right lung collapse
Bilateral pleural effusion (LT > RT)

label: Cardiomegaly
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Inadequate inspiratory effort. Significant rotation to the right is also observed.
The suboptimal inspiratory effort and significant rotation possibly account for apparent cardiomegaly (CTR = 0.56 ).
The mediastinum, hila and pulmonary vasculature are normal.
No focal lung lesion is seen.
There is blunting of the right costophrenic sulcus. There is also thickening of the transverse fisssure.
The left costophrenic sulcus and hemidiaphragm are preserved.
Degenerative changes of the lower thoracic vertebrae are observed.
The peripheral soft tissues are normal.
CONCLUSION
Mild right pleural effusion

report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH  REPORT
Near complete opacification of the right hemithorax is noted with nodular opacities all over the left hemithorax and silhouetting of the right hemidiaphragm and right heart shadows.
No significant shift of the mediastinum, suggestive of right lung collapse.
The left hemidiaphragm and costophrenic recess are normal.
Extra pulmonary soft tissues are normal.
Conclusion
Metastatic lung disease with massive right sided pleural effusion.
Miliary tuberculosis.

label: Cardiomegaly
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Widespread interstitial opacities are seen over both lung fields with multifocal airspace opacities on the right 
A homogeneous opacity with silhouetting the lateral aspect of the right  hemi diaphragm is seen 
The cardiac shadow is enlarged . The  cardiothoracic ratio is 57%.
Widening of  the vascular pedicure is seen.
The left hemi diaphragm and ribs   appear normal. 
Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine is seen .
Central venous line projected over the right hemithorax  with tip in the region of ipsilateral atrium.

Impression: features suggest  ;
1.Right sided acute respiratory distress syndrome with  associated pleural effusion .
 2. Left sided  Interstitial pneumonic changes 
3.Cardiomegaly ? Cause
Recommend right lateral chest radiograph

label: Atelectasis
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Findings:
Diffuse reticulonodular opacities seen involving the left hemithorax.
Areas of hypertransradiancy devoid of lung markings are noted at the periphery of the right hemithorax, indicative of pneumothorax. Adjacent homogenous opacity noted, suggestive of collapsed right lung.
The cardiac shadow is normal in size (CTR - 0.47) and contour.
The costophrenic sulci and hemidiaphragms are preserved.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
Impression:
Pulmonary TB complicated by right pneumothorax and lung collapse

report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Findings:
The cardiac shadow is enlarged (CTR - 0.55) with a left ventricular preponderance.
There is unfolding of the aorta arch.
The mediastinum, hila and pulmonary vasculature are normal.
No focal lung lesion is seen.
The costophrenic sulci and hemidiaphragms are preserved.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
Dorsal spine degenerative changes noted. Marked joint space narrowing with subchondral sclerosis, cysts and marginal osteophytes noted at the glenohumeral joints bilaterally.
Impression:
Features suggestive of hypertensive heart disease 
Osteonecrosis of the gleno-humeral joint with secondary osteoarthritis (Ficat and Arlet IV)
ECG advised

report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
There is a large area of hyperlucency with no visible lung markings seen surrounding the right lung and displacing it medially, in keeping with a pneumothorax. There is associated collapse of the right lung. There is associated widening of the right intercostal spaces and subtle depression of the right hemidiaphragm. However, there is no significant shift of the mediastinum to the left.
The heart is normal in size and contour (CTR = 0.45).
The mediastinum, hila and pulmonary vasculature are also normal.
No focal lung lesion is seen.
The left costophrenic sulcus and hemidiaphragm are preserved.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
CONCLUSION
Features are those of a right tension pneumothorax

report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Findings:
The cardiac shadow is normal in size (CTR - 0.48) and contour.
Cavitary lesions are seen bilaterally, at the right upper lung zone (3.5cm x 1.9cm) as well as the left middle lung zone (5.7cm x 5.1) on a background inhomogenous streaky opacities seen predominantly in right upper and left middle and lower lung zones.

The right costophrenic sulcus and hemidiaphragm is preserved.
Degenerative thoracic spine changes are noted.
The peripheral soft tissues are normal.
Impression:
Features suggestive of pulmonary Tuberculosis
Correlate clinically

label: Atelectasis
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPHY REPORT
Comments
There is inhomogenous opacity of soft tissue density with air bronchogram noted in the medial aspect of the right lung filed silhouetting the right cardiac margin. There are associated mediastinal and subtle tracheal deviation to the ipsilateral side with consequent marked reduction in the thoracic volume.
right sided subtle rib crowding is also noted suggestive of atelectasis.
There is lobulated contour of the left hilar region suggestive of hilar adenopathy.
There is associated compensatory hyper transradiance of the left lung.
No pleural effusion.
Impression
Features are suggestive of right lobar pneumonia with atelectasis r/o pulmonary tuberculosis.
Chest CT advised

CHEST RADIOGRAPHY REPORT
Comments
Interval improvement compared to previous radiograph.
Homogenous opacity seen in the right middle and lower lung zones with air-fluid level tracking up ipsilateral 3rd anterior rib laterally.

There is mottled soft tissue with increased density seen in the right lateral chest wall.

The left lung field is preserved.
Left costophrenic sulcus and hemidiaphragm are preserved
The bones of the thorax appear normal.
Impression
Moderate right hemothorax.

Interstitial pneumonic changes ? Pulmonary Tuberculosis, ?? Sarcoidosis

The costophrenic sulci and hemidiaphragms are preserved.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
Impression:
Cardiomegaly with aortic unfolding

label: Atelectasis
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT 
Image is rotated to the left
The heart  appears normal in size
Absence of  vascular markings in the left lower lung zone with air fluid level and  crowding of left upper pulmonary  vascular markings.
There is associated crowding of the ribs on the left.
Subtle mediastinal shift to the left  and mild  compensatory hyperinflation of the right lung noted
The costophrenic and cardiophrenic angles are also preserved on the right
The peripheral soft tissues are normal .
CONCLUSION
Left lung collapse with hydropneumothorax

report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Rotated to the left.
The heart shadow is enlarged with left ventricular configuration. The cardiothoraic ratio is 0.64.
The aorta is unfolded.
There is prominence of the pulmonary vascular pedicles.
There are streaky opacities noted in the right middle and part of the lower lung  zone with haziness of the right cardiac border.
There is blunting of the right costophrenic sulcus.
The left costophrenic sulcus is free.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
CONCLUSION
Features are suggestive of hypertensive heart disease with right pneumonic changes  and minimal right pleural effusion

label: Degenerative_Structural_Changes
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH  REPORT
Diffuse reticular opacities are noted in both lung zones.
There is widening of the mediastinum with aortic unfolding and subtle outward convexity of the descending aorta giving appearance of aortic aneurysm (5.48cm)
 Elevated left diaphragm noted.
Subchondral sclerosis of the glenoid is noted. There is irregularity of the humeral head bilaterally with multiple area of lucencies

The cardiac shadows are within normal limits.
Normal pulmonary vasculature is seen.
Both hilar angles are preserved.
Both costophrenic recesses and hemidiaphragms are normal.
The bones of the thorax appear normal.
Extra pulmonary soft tissues are normal.
Conclusion:  
Widened mediastinum ? Aortic aneurysm- Recommendation: Urgent CT Chest
Shoulder osteoarthritis

Recommendation: USS Abdomen in view of left diaphragm elevation.

label: Atelectasis
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Comments
There are fibrotic streaks noted within the left lung field tenting the diaphragm.
The left upper lobe is collapsed with ipsilateral mediastinal shift.
There are old granulomatous nodules observed in the left middle lung zone
No focal lesion is seen in the right lung field.
The heart is normal in size and contour (CTR is 0.44).
Normal pulmonary vasculature is seen.
Both hilar angles are preserved.
The costophrenic sulci are preserved.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
Impression
Features suggest pulmonary tuberculosis
Gene Xpert and chest CT correlation are advised.

label: Degenerative_Structural_Changes
report_text:
AP CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
The heart shadow appears enlarged (CTR is 0.55).
There is unfolding of the aorta.
The hila appear normal.
There is cephalization of the pulmonary vessels.
A homogenous opacity is observed blunting the right costophrenic sulcus with associated tracking into the transverse fissure and mediastinum, suggestive of right pleural effusion.
An area of inhomogenous consolidation is also observed in the right middle and lower lung zone region, silhouetting the right hemidiaphragm. There is background haziness of the right lung field.
Thin transverse peripheral lines extending to the pleura are observed, suggestive of Kerley B lines. 
The left costophrenic sulcus and hemidiaphragm are preserved.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
CONCLUSION
Features are suggestive of congestive cardiac failure, kiv underlying pneumonic changes

label: Atelectasis
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
There is mild reduction in the left lung volume with subtle ipsilateral mediastinal shift.
There is a near-homogenous opacity with internal air lucencies in the left lower and middle lung zones, silhouetting the left hemidiaphragm and cardiac border, suggestive of a left pleural effusion
There is also an area of hyperlucency and absent lung markings seen lateral to the left lung. suggestive of a pneumothorax.
Fibrocavitary lesions are noted in the collapsed left upper lobe. Reticulonodular opacities are seen in the right upper lobe.
Consolidative changes are observed in the left lower lobe.
The heart size is indeterminate.
The mediastinum, hila and pulmonary vasculature are normal.
The right costophrenic sulcus and hemidiaphragm are preserved.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
CONCLUSION
Known TB patient with left-sided hydropneumothorax and collapse
Chest CT advised for further evaluation

label: Degenerative_Structural_Changes
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPHY REPORT
Homogenous opacities are noted in both lower lung zones with associated lamellar tracking, obscuring the hemidiaphragm and cardiac border on the left, suggestive of bilateral pleural effusion (L>R).
Fluffy perihilar opacities on the right, suggestive of alveolar pulmonary edema.
There is thickening of the transverse tissue.
The heart size is indeterminate.
Aortic unfolding is noted.
Normal pulmonary vasculature is seen, and both hilar angles are preserved.
The bones of the thorax and extra-pulmonary soft tissues are normal.
Finding:
Features suggestive of congestive cardiac failure.

evidence_phrases: effusion; blunting of the right costophrenic; hydropneumothorax
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
There is reduction in volume and dense fibrotic changes in the left lung with associated shift of the mediastinum to the ipsilateral side, and hyperinflation of the right lung. There is also associated mild crowding of the left ribs.
Air lucencies with air-fluid levels inferiorly are noted in the left middle and lower lung zones suggestive of hydropneumothorax.
Small cavities are also noted in the left upper lung zone.
Linear fibrotic and reticular opacities are seen in the right upper lung zone.
There is blunting of the right costophrenic sulcus, suggestive of right pleural effusion.
The heart size is indeterminate.
The right hilum and pulmonary vasculature appear normal.
The peripheral soft tissues are normal.
CONCLUSION
Features are those of complicated PTB

Tubular artifact, suggestive of central venous line, is seen with tip at the level of T7, ~3.5cm below the carina, in the region of the right atrium.
The left costophrenic sulcus and hemidiaphragm is preserved.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
Impression:
Features suggestive of Pulmonary TB with pneumonic consolidation

report_text:

Central venous line is seen projected over the right hemithorax with tip about 4.8cm below the carina in the region of the right atrium.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
Impression:
Bilateral mild pleural effusion

Bilateral focal pulmonary edema
Mild right pleural effusion
Aortic unfolding with wall calcification.
Prominent pulmonary vasculature

Impression
Hypertensive heart disease with mild right pleural effusion

Impression
Features suggestive of loculated left pleural effusion with pneumonic changes

Image is markedly rotated to the left
Cardiac shadow, hilar, mediastinum and left costophrenic angle cannot be adequately assessed as a result

Multiple lytic lesions noted on the  visualized thoracic skeleton with pathologic fractures of the 4th to 7th ribs
CONCLUSION
Known breast cancer patient with features suggestive of metastatic bone disease with pathologic fractures
CHEST CT SCAN RECOMMENDED

AP radiograph.
Oxygen tube artefact is projected over the left upper hemithorax and lower neck.
Widespread multiple rounded opacities of varying sizes are visualized in both lung fields.
Partial collapse-consolidation noted in the right middle and lower lung zones.
The cardiac shadow appears enlarged. The cardiothoracic ratio is 0.54.
The aorta is unfolded.
Blunting of the right costophenic angle is seen.
The left costophrenic sulcus, diaphragm, bony thorax and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
CONCLUSION
Features are in keeping with pulmonary metastases (cannonball)

label: Degenerative_Structural_Changes
report_text:
AP CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT===PRELIMINARY====
Patchy air space opacities seen predominantly in both middle and right lower lung zones.
Homogeneous opacity seen in the right lower lung zone, obliterating the ipsilateral costophrenic sulcus and hemidiaphragm and tracking upto ipsilateral 4th posterior rib  laterally.

Curvilinear calcification of the aortic wall noted.
The heart is normal in size and contour (CTR 0.56)
The mediastinum, hila and pulmonary vasculature are also normal.

The left costophrenic sulcus and hemidiaphragm are preserved.

Dorsal spondylosis noted.
Tubal artefact's tip seen at the level of right 8th parasagittal vertebral body. Also, comma shape soft tissue density with calcific rim abut the drain's tip medially (? Sutured soft tissue)
Drain tubes' tip seen at the level of right 6th posterior rib intercostal space
CONCLUSION
Kindly correlate with other clinical parameters.

label: Atelectasis
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Comparison: Chest Xray [REDACTED_ID]
Findings:
Inomogenous opacity is seen involving the left lower lung zone silhouetting the left cardiac border and hemidiaphragm with lamellar tracking along the left lateral chest wall.
Consolidative changes noted in the left middle lung zone.
There is reduction in the left lung volume.
Cardiac size indeterminate (left margin obscured by the aforementioned opacity).
Curvilinear calcification of the aortic knob noted, consistent with atherosclerosis.
Stomach bubble seen in the thoracic compartment most probably due to elevated left hemidiaphragm.
The right costophrenic sulcus is preserved.
The soft tissues appear within normal limits.
The bony thorax is intact.
Impression:
Left pleural effusion with consolidative collapse
When compared with the previous study (24/09/24), there is no significant interval change.

The right pulmonary vasculature is normal.
The right costophrenic sulcus and hemidiaphragm are preserved.
The bony rib cage is normal.

Left mediastinal mass
Left hydropneumothorax

label: Cardiomegaly
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPHY REPORT
Clinical information
To check for central line
Comparison
No previous exams available.
Comments
The CVC projects to the left of the vertebral column(crossing the midline) at the level of the left sternoclavicular joint.
No pneumothorax or pleural collection seen. 
The lung fields are clear.
There is a sub-diaphragmatic lucency with no haustral markings
The heart shows enlarged shadow(CTR: 59%)
Mediastinal shadows are within normal limits.
The bones of the thorax appear normal.
Impression
Displaced / Misplaced CVC
Sub-diaphragmatic lucency? pneumoperitoneum
Cardiomegaly
*A repeated X radiograph shows the CVC at the level of the distal one third of the right clavicle, 21.46mm across the right sternal margin, at an acute angle inferiorly.
If the CVC is functional with hemodynamic stability, a persistent left SVC might be the likely diagnosis

report_text:
PRELIMINARY REPORT
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
There is homogenous opacification of the left thoracic cavity up to the middle zone, silhouetting the left hemi-diaphragm and the left heart border. There is a 67.1x52.5mm left hilar opaque mass which does not obscure the main bronchus and the vasculature.
There is mild deviation of the trachea to the left.
There is a soft tissue density measuring 56.9x42.7mm in the lateral middle lobe of the right lung with well defined borders.
The heart contour cannot be delineated.
The mediastinum, hila and pulmonary vasculature are poorly visualized on the left, but normal on the right. Soft tissue irregular opacities in the right chest wall is noted
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
FINDINGS:
Right soft tissue density
Left hilar mass? location
Left pleural effusion
A left lateral image and a CT chest is advised for a comprehensive report

report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
There is a homogenous opacity with associated lamellar tracking observed in the right middle and lower lung zones, silhouetting the right cardiac border and ipsilateral hemidiaphragm.
A chest tube artefact is observed within the right hemithorax entering through the lateral 7th intercostal space with Its tip seen to lie in the 11th intercostal space medially. Adjacent soft tissue emphysema is noted.
Bilateral fluffy perihilar airspace opacities are noted giving the batwing appearance.
There is blunting of the left costophrenic sulcus.
The heart size is indeterminate, however there is mitralisation of the left heart border.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
CONCLUSION
Overall appearances are those of cardiac decompensation with bilateral pulmonary edema and moderate right pleural effusion

There is a homogenous opacity with associated lamellar tracking observed in the right middle and lower lung zones, silhouetting the right cardiac border and ipsilateral hemidiaphragm.
A chest tube artefact is observed within the right hemithorax entering through the lateral 7th intercostal space with Its tip seen to lie in the 11th intercostal space medially. Adjacent soft tissue emphysema is noted.
Bilateral fluffy perihilar airspace opacities are noted giving the batwing appearance.
There is blunting of the left costophrenic sulcus.
The heart size is indeterminate, however there is mitralisation of the left heart border.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
CONCLUSION
Overall appearances are those of cardiac decompensation with bilateral pulmonary edema and moderate right pleural effusion

report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
There is a homogenous opacity in the right lower lung zone with lamellar tracking up to the level of the 4th posterior intercostal space. A chest tube artefact is seen in the right hemithorax with its tip projected at the level of the 6th posterior rib. There is thickening of the transverse fissure with the homogenous opacity.
There is a homogenous opacity in the left lower lung zone with lamellar tracking up to the 6th posterior intercostal space. There is a chest tube artefact with its tip projected at the level of the 10th posterior rib.
Prominent vascular markings are observed bilaterally.
The cardiac shadow is markedly enlarged with a globular configuration. The cardiothoracic ratio is 0.74.
The bony rib cage is normal.
There is a mild surgical emphysema noted in the extrathoracic soft tissue on the left.
CONCLUSION
Massive cardiomegaly r/o pericardial effusion, with bilateral pleural effusion, in a patient with background CCF - Echocardiography is advised

label: Pleural_Effusion
evidence_phrases: effusion; effusion; silhouetting the right hemnidiaphragm
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
There is a near-homogenous opacity in the right lower lung zone silhouetting the right hemnidiaphragm, with associated lamellar tracking. When compared with the previous film, there is slight increase in the volume of the pleural effusion.
There is a transverse linear opacity in the right middle lung zone ?thickened transverse fissure. Patchy opacities are noted in the right lower lung zone.
Subtle reticular opacities are also noted in the left lower lung zone.
The heart is normal in size and contour.
The mediastinum, hila and pulmonary vasculature are also normal.
The left costophrenic sulcus, left hemidiaphragm and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
Numerous bridging osteophytes are observed on the lateral margins of the T5 to T12 vertebral bodies.
CONCLUSION
Right sided pleural effusion with interval increase when compared with the last radiograph (15/10/24)

label: Cardiomegaly
report_text:
Preliminary report
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
There is homogenous opacity of the lower right lung zone, silhouetting the right hemi-diaphragm and blunting the right costophrenic angle.
There is multiple reticular opacities in both lung fields with upper lobe increased vascular markings. There is also subtle blunting of the left costophrenic angle
Cardiomegaly(63% CTR) with left ventricular preponderance is noted with aortic unfolding
There is an opaque tubular artifact with its tip in the right 4th intercoastal space(region of the SVC). 
The mediastinum, hila and pulmonary vasculature are also normal.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
CONCLUSION
Cardiomegaly with aortic unfolding with upper lobe diversion, suggestive of cardiac failure
Bilateral basal pleural effusion(right >>>left)
?Pneumonia
CTPA is advised to rule out PE

label: Cardiomegaly
report_text:
PRELIMINARY REPORT
CHEST X RAY REPORT
The heart is enlarged with a globular outline and a CTR of 70%
It left cardiac border extends to the left chest wall. There is bibasal minimal effusion blunting the costophrenic angles bilaterally and silhouetting the left hemidiaphragm, as well as thickened interlobular septa seen in the right right lung base(kerly B lines)
The aorto-pulmonary concavity is preserved.
The mediastinum and midline structures are unremarkable.
The chest wall bones and overlying soft tissue are normal.
Impression:
Cardiomegaly with features suggestive of heart failure
echocardiography and ecg might be helpful

label: Cardiomegaly
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Comparison: Chest Xray 15/11/24
Findings:
Near homogenous opacity is seen involving the right middle and lower lung zones with lamellar tracking along the inner costal wall up to the lateral aspect of the 2nd rib.
There is mild reduction in cardiac shadow (CTR - 0.66) when compared to the previous acquired image (CTR - 0.69).
Left perihilar opacities are noted with associated haziness of both lung fields.
There is blunting of the left costophrenic sulcus.
Pericardiostomy catheter is noted with tip over the region of the left ventricle.
Central venous line artifact seen projected over the right hemithorax with tip at the level of T8, ~10cm below the carina, in the region of the right atrium.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
Impression:
Known patient with pericardial effusion with:
Mild reduction in cardiac size
Bilateral pleural effusion (Right >> Left)

report_text:
AP CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT

The cardiac shadow is enlarged (CTR - 0.75) with a multichamber configuration.
The left lung field is obscured by the cardiac shadow.
The costophrenic sulci and hemidiaphragms are preserved.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
Impression:
Known case of congenital heart disease with features suggestive of bronchopneumonia

label: Cardiomegaly
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Findings
A large fibro cavitary lesion involving the upper and middle left lung field, devoid of lung markings , with a large air-fluid level at the lower lung zone is seen, obliterating the ipsilateral hemidiaphragm and costophrenic sulcus.
Peripheral amorphous calcific densities and fibrotic streaks are also noted in the left lung field along the apical region and middle lung zones. 
Haziness of the right lung field is noted.
Right perihilar opacity with convex outer margin is noted, ? lymphadenopathy.
Mild right tracheal and mediastinal deviation is noted
The cardiac shadow is enlarged (CTR - 0.67).
The right costophrenic sulcus, hemidiaphragm, bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are  within normal limits.
Features include: 
1. Huge left fibro-cavitary lesion with hydropneumothorax.
2. Right interstitial pneumonic changes 
3. Right hilar fullness ? Lymphadenopathy
Suggestive features of Pulmonary Koch’s disease

label: Cardiomegaly
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPHY REPORT
Clinical information
Aspiration pneumonitis

Comparison
No previous exams available.

Comments
There is cardiomegaly (CTR- 65%) of multichamber enlargement with associated aortic unfolding and upper lobe blood diversion.
There are regions of airspace opacification with interlobular septal lines, as well as thickening of the minor fissure.
There is ill-definition of the left hemidiaphragm and left costophrenic sulcus.

The bones of the thorax appear normal.

Impression
Features are those of congestive cardiac failure with pneumonic consolidation.

Echocardiography and ECG advised for further evaluation.

Homogeneous opacity seen in the left lower lung zone, obscuring the costophrenic sulcus and hemidiaphragm and tracking upto the 5th posterior rib laterally.

The right costophrenic sulcus and hemidiaphragm are preserved.
The bony rib cage is normal.
CONCLUSION
Moderate left pleural effusion

The heart shadow is enlarged. The cardiothoracic ratio is 0.59.
There is a triangular perihilar region of consolidation in the right upper lung zone limited inferior by the transverse fissure. Fluffy opacities are also noted in the left lower lung zone.
Mild peribronchial cuffing is observed in both lower lung zones.
There is mild deviation of the trachea to the right.
There is prominence of the pulmonary markings bilaterally, with cephalization of the pulmonary vasculature.
The costophrenic sulci and hemidiaphragms are preserved.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
Tubal artefacts are observed overlying the chest wall.
CONCLUSION
CT chest is advised for further evaluation

Features suggestive of PTB, with left lung collapse and pleural effusion

CONCLUSION
Left tension hydropneumothorax

Inadequate inspiratory effort is observed.

There is a transverse linear opacity observed in the left lower lung zone, suspicious for plate atelectasis from inadequate inspiration.
The costophrenic sulci and hemidiaphragms are preserved.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
CONCLUSION

CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT

The heart size is indeterminate.
The mediastinum, hila and pulmonary vasculature are normal.

The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
CONCLUSION
Moderate right pleural effusion poste chest tube insertion
